title: Why does JSONExtractString on a numeric key fail on comparison with a numeric literal?
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Question
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/185ebc7f-d65b-4c78-854a-68e43e10fa86
Please help me find a reson for this.
When I do `select '200' > 100` in clickhouse this works by converting to a common integer or float supertype.
But when I do this:
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS demo_ts;
CREATE TABLE demo_ts
(
id     UInt32,
labels String
)
ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO demo_ts VALUES
(1, '{"http.status_code":"200"}'),
(2, '{"http.status_code":"503"}'),
(4, '{"http.status_code":"208"}');
SELECT
id,
JSONExtractString(labels, 'http.status_code') AS sc
FROM demo_ts
ORDER BY id;
SELECT '200' > 100.0; -- this works
SELECT count(*)
FROM demo_ts
WHERE JSONHas(labels, 'http.status_code')
AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'http.status_code') > 200.0; -- this does not work even if we have JSONHas why?
I even have JSONHas check. And all the values of JSONExtractString can be converted to numeric values.
One more follow up question,
https://clickhouse.com/docs/operations/analyzer?utm_source=chatgpt.com#incompatible-function-arguments-types
This mentions
In the new analyzer, type inference happens during initial query analysis. This change means that type checks are done before short-circuit evaluation; thus, the if function arguments must always have a common supertype.
What does this mean for and condition?
key_bug_features: JSONExtractString comparison with numeric literal fails
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE demo_ts (id UInt32, labels String) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT count(*) FROM demo_ts WHERE JSONHas(labels, 'http.status_code') AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'http.status_code') > '100';
SELECT id, JSONExtractString(labels, 'http.status_code') AS sc FROM demo_ts WHERE JSONExtractString(labels, 'http.status_code') IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sc;
